notes: The root-discovery and observation-storage reviews each found blocking issues in their reviewed commits, but those storage/query problems were superseded by later milestone commits. The remaining active blocker is the row_id compatibility break reported on claude-source-aware-platform-detail-api.

blockingIssues.description: Commit 2eae38e makes duplicate Claude detail requests require row_id, but the committed callers still open/load detail by plain skillId only (`src/pages/PlatformView.tsx`, `src/components/skill/SkillDetailView.tsx`, `src/stores/skillDetailStore.ts`). Opening a duplicate Claude row therefore fails instead of preserving source-specific detail state.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When backend IPC payloads change, require the worker to also run the frontend-targeted tests plus `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint`, not just Rust-targeted tests and clippy.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `claude-source-aware-platform-detail-api` found a committed backend contract change that breaks existing frontend callers, and the mission guidance/services manifest already required frontend-targeted validation for these payload changes.

rejectedObservations.observation: The backend worker skill/procedure lacks guidance for producing a scoped feature commit when the shared mission workspace is already dirty from unrelated work.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in mission AGENTS.md, which already tells workers not to clean/stash unrelated dirty files and to commit only feature-owned files.

rejectedObservations.observation: The worker marked `followedProcedure` true even though the reviewed feature stored `row_id` internally without exposing it on the reviewed API surface.
rejectedObservations.reason: feature-specific reviewer feedback rather than a durable shared-state rule or repository knowledge update.
